questions.question: Consider a binary classification problem where there are two classes A and B. The prior probabilities of these classes are P(A) = 0.6 and P(B) = 0.4. Given a feature vector X, the posterior probabilities of the classes are P(A|X) = 0.8 and P(B|X) = 0.2. Calculate the posterior probabilities of the classes using Bayes' theorem. (5 marks)

A spam filter uses a Bayes model to classify emails as spam or not spam. The prior probability of a spam email is P(S) = 0.1 and the probability of a not spam email is P(NS) = 0.9. Given the feature vector of the email, calculate the posterior probability of it being spam using Bayes' theorem. (5 marks)

A binary classification problem has 10 features and 1000 training samples. If the prior probabilities of the two classes are P(A) = 0.5 and P(B) = 0.5, and the posterior probabilities of the classes given the feature vectors are P(A|X) = 0.7 and P(B|X) = 0.3, calculate the likelihood of the samples using Bayes' theorem. (10 marks)
